summary: date-fns-lite postinstall beacons host/user identifiers to attacker-controlled zk959.eyes.sh
details: date-fns-lite@1.0.0 is a typosquat of the widely-used date-fns package. Its postinstall.js collects installer identifiers — os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and process.platform — encodes them into a subdomain of the hardcoded attacker-controlled domain zk959.eyes.sh (e.g. `${hostname}-${username}-${platform}.rw5fooe1.zk959.eyes.sh`), and triggers resolution/HTTP callbacks via dns.resolve4 and child_process.execSync of curl/wget/nslookup/host/dig against that subdomain. This fires unconditionally on `npm install` with no disclosure, consent, or configuration, exfiltrating installer identity to the attacker on every install. The thin date-formatting surface exists only to make the package plausible to installers who mistype date-fns.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
